Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Statements Of Damages, For Monetary, Issue, Evidentiary, And Terminating Sanctions For Violations Of Discovery Statutes
Off Calendar Oct-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances on TUE-OCT-08-2013:
Russell A. Robinson, Esq. [415.255.0462] appearing (at 9:00 a.m.) for Defendants ROYAL OAK BAR and ARES PAPAGEORGE;
DeWitt M. Lacy, Esq. of Law Offices of John L. Burris [510.839.5200] appeared after 9:20AM for plaintiffs MICHEAL BEAL and ASHLEY JACKSON

At 9:07 a.m., first calling of the case, the Court adopts its tentative ruling re:
Defendants ROYAL OAK BAR'S and ARES PAPAGEORGE'S Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Statements Of Damages, For Monetary, Issue, Evidentiary, And Terminating Sanctions For Violations Of Discovery Statutes.
The Pro Tem Judge had issued the following tentative ruling: IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFFS HAVE FILED THE REQUESTED STATEMENT OF DAMAGES WHICH MAKES IT UNNECESSARY TO ISSUE AN ORDER FOR THEM TO DO SO. DEFENDANTS REQUEST FOR EVIDENTIARY AND TERMINATING SANCTIONS IS DENIED; MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1860.00 AGAINST PLAINTIFFS' ATTORNEYS AS NO JUSTIFICATION WAS PROVIDED AS TO WHY THEY FAILED TO FILE THE REQUESTED STATEMENTS OF DAMAGES PRIOR TO THE FILING OF DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL. Order signed in open court.

At 9:25 a.m., outside the presence of defense counsel, the Court recalls the motion and hears argument by plaintiffs' counsel. Plaintiffs' counsel did not contest the tentative ruling by 4PM as required on October 7, 2013. Pursuant to the Court's tentative ruling: "IF THE TENTATIVE RULING IS NOT CONTSTED, THE PARTIES ARE DEEMED TO HAVE STIPULATED TO THE PRO TEM HEARING THE MOTION AND THE PRO TEM WILL SIGN AN ORDER CONFIRMING THE TENTATIVE RULING." The Court re-confirms its adopted tentative ruling. Judge Pro Tem: Paul A. Renne; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)
